page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd is a compute-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs across 12 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a base speed of 2.3 GHz and supported by a 504 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 90.0 GB of RAM, providing a allocation of 3.75 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 1343 GB of NVMe SSD capacity spread across four local drives. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 10%. Multi-core performance is strong, as demonstrated by Geekbench multi-core and image processing benchmarks falling in the top 25%. For language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds on small 135M models, though performance drops significantly to the bottom 10% for text generation on large 70B models. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-24-lssd offers a balanced resource density, combining high-performance compute with fast, bundled local NVMe storage. This eliminates the need for external storage attachments for IOPS-heavy tasks, though the fixed memory-to-vCPU ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications. This server is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, single-threaded data processing, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd is a compute-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor at 2.3 GHz. It includes 90.0 GB of RAM and 1343 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, alongside strong multi-core capabilities and high static web serving throughput. However, memory bandwidth and large-model LLM text generation remain average to poor. This configuration is highly efficient for workloads requiring fast local storage and strong single-core compute, such as web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd is a compute-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 90 GB of RAM, and 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 90 GB RAM, and 1343 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing with high-speed bundled NVMe storage for demanding single-core and web-serving workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 504 MB L3 cache
90.0 GB of system memory providing 3.75 GB per core
1343 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across four drives
Top-tier single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks
Well-suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
